title: Holiday Wreath Decorating
summary: Everyone is invited to decorate the wreaths that will adorn Block Island’s public buildings and utility poles during the holiday season. Please join the Block Island Gardeners. Bring gardening gloves and clippers. Help in distributing wreaths to town buildings is also appreciated. Light refreshments will be provided. Please contact Maude at (203) 233-4889 with any questions.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Rabies Clinic
summary: Chris Hannafin, DVM, of New Shoreham Veterinary Services will sponsor a rabies vaccination clinic for dogs, cats, and ferrets. In the meeting room of the Police Station. Cost is $12.00 for each vaccination (cash or check only). All dogs must be leashed and all cats and ferrets must be in carriers. Proof of a prior rabies vaccination (certificate) must be shown in order to receive a multi-year booster shot. For questions, contact Dr. Hannafin at the practice at 401-783-7789.
content: Chris Hannafin, DVM, of New Shoreham Veterinary Services will sponsor a rabies vaccination clinic for dogs, cats, and ferrets. In the meeting room of the Police Station. Cost is $12.00 for each vaccination (cash or check only). All dogs must be leashed and all cats and ferrets must be in carriers. Proof of a prior rabies vaccination (certificate) must be shown in order to receive a multi-year booster shot. For questions, contact Dr. Hannafin at the practice at 401-783-7789.

summary: Description:
content:
Description:

This half marathon will begin and end at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion. The cost is $50 (non refundable) and T-shirts are guaranteed to all pre-race registrants. There are medals awarded to all finishers!

Register online.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 34th Annual Holiday Shopping Stroll
summary: The Holiday Stroll is a Block Island tradition happening again this year from Friday, November 28th through Sunday, Nov. 30th. Enjoy a weekend - or day - on Block Island, with fun, festivities, and relaxed & friendly shopping.  The Shopping Stroll kicks off the holiday season with the town adorned in festive decorations, a gift basket raffle, and local shops offering incredible sales beginning on Friday morning! For more info on stroll festivities, accommodations, dining, and shopping, please check out our brochure here.
content:
The Holiday Stroll is a Block Island tradition happening again this year from Friday, November 28th through Sunday, Nov. 30th. Enjoy a weekend - or day - on Block Island, with fun, festivities, and relaxed & friendly shopping.  The Shopping Stroll kicks off the holiday season with the town adorned in festive decorations, a gift basket raffle, and local shops offering incredible sales beginning on Friday morning! For more info on stroll festivities, accommodations, dining, and shopping, please check out our brochure here.

Note that there will be Traditional and High Speed ferry service from Pt.Judith over the Holiday Stroll weekend! For schedule information see here:
https://www.blockislandferry.com/schedules-and-fares/

author: Austin Crawford
title: Socktober Event!
summary: The New Shoreham Police Department is excited to announce our first ever "Socktober" event. Socktober is a month long community sock drive where new, packaged socks can be dropped off at clearly marked boxes located at Washington Trust Bank, Town Hall, and the Island Free Library.  All sizes of socks are welcome!  The sock collections will go to the Harbor Church and the BI School.
content:
The New Shoreham Police Department is excited to announce our first ever "Socktober" event. Socktober is a month long community sock drive where new, packaged socks can be dropped off at clearly marked boxes located at Washington Trust Bank, Town Hall, and the Island Free Library.  All sizes of socks are welcome!  The sock collections will go to the Harbor Church and the BI School. 

Please contact Doug or Beth at the New Shoreham Police Department with any questions. Hope to make our first Socktober a successful undertaking!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Pride 2025
summary: Sunday, September 14th
content:
Sunday, September 14th

 

Start your day connecting to nature with The Nature Conservancy’s Queerness in Nature program at Vail from 11:00 am–1:00 pm — a perfect way to recharge and reflect. “When we look to nature we find a diverse spectrum of gender expressions and sexual behaviors, because nature is queer and being queer is natural”. Wrap up Pride weekend at the Secret Garden Party Brunch at a surprise location at 1:00 pm — a final chance to toast, dance, and celebrate community.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Pride 2025
content:
Saturday, September 13th

 

Kick off the morning with Rainbow Story Hour at the Island Free Library from 10:00–11:00 am, where we will delight with stories and sparkle. Book a 15-min portrait session with Cole Caswell behind The Darius Inn made using the historic wet-plate collodion process (slots from 10:00-3:00 PM behind the Darius Inn). Then it’s time to strut your stuff at the Pride March, starting at Ocean View Pavilion at 11:30 am (steps off at 12:20 pm). Bring all the extravaganza and make some noise! After you march, refuel at Brunch & Performances at The Yellow Kittens from 1:00–3:00 pm with DJ Libre spinning the tunes and more performances — all family-friendly and fabulous! Later, explore your inner artist at the Life Drawing Class at Block Island Beach House from 4:00–5:00 pm. Finish the day with a Soundwaves Beach Movie film screening of The Birdcage (1996) under the stars starting at 6:30 pm. Bring chairs, blankets, and your best movie snacks!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Pride 2025
summary: Friday, September 12th
content:
Friday, September 12th


Book a 15-min portrait session with Cole Caswell behind The Darius Innmade using the historic wet-plate collodion process. Mellow out with us for All-Levels Gentle Yoga & Peace and Love Meditation on the beach below the Block Island Beach House, from 2:00–3:15 pm with Queer Yogi Bailey Quinlan of Woo-Woo Crew. Breathe, stretch, and reconnect with yourself in this grounding practice designed for every body and every level and a short Tangerine meditation from the Plum Village School of Zen Meditation. Start your engines at the Rainbow Tie-Dye Party at The Traveling Seamstress from 4:00–6:00 pm. Sling those colors like you mean it — yasss darling! Next, dab your lips and roll on over to Unicorn Bingo at the Island Free Library from 5:00–6:00 pm. Grab your cards and get ready for some fierce laughs and fabulous prizes! When you're warmed up, slip into something sultry for the Jazz Bar Speakeasy at Persephone's Kitchen from 6:00–8:30 pm. Smooth jazz and tarot Readings ($20-10 min slots) from Bailey Quinlan of Woo-Woo Crew. Finally, close out the night by dancing under the disco ball at the Liquid Dancefloor Drag Party at Captain Nick’s from 9:00 pm–1:00 am (21+). Featuring beats by DJ Chardanaynay and performances by Rhode Island drag royalty.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Sandra Swan at Block Island Historical Society
summary: The Block Island Historical Society, Jessie Edwards, and Island collectors are hosting a retrospective exhibit of Sandra Swan’s work August 7-9 at the Historical Society. Also, join us for a conversation with Sandra on August 7 at 7:00 and August 8 at 5:00.
content: The Block Island Historical Society, Jessie Edwards, and Island collectors are hosting a retrospective exhibit of Sandra Swan’s work August 7-9 at the Historical Society. Also, join us for a conversation with Sandra on August 7 at 7:00 and August 8 at 5:00.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Christmas in July at Champlin's
content:
​

Enjoy the Holiday Spirit and the warmth of summer. Stop by to check out the decorated boats, our festive food and beverage specials and a special visit from Santa and his helpers! DJ party at the Dock Bar from 9:00pm to Midnight. 


Holiday boat decorating contest

Join the fun & deck out your boat for a chance to win:

1st place: $3,000 gift certificate

2nd place: $1,500 gift certificate

3rd place: $500 gift certificate 

Main dock reserved for participants.
Winners announced during the traders party.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tri-State Canyon Shootout - Fishing Tournament at Champlin's
summary: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. This year the tournament will be based out of Champlin's. So come down and check out the boats and the fish. The afternoon weigh-ins are not to be missed!
content: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. This year the tournament will be based out of Champlin's. So come down and check out the boats and the fish. The afternoon weigh-ins are not to be missed!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Where-on-Earth Bird Walk
summary: Where-on-Earth Bird Walk
content:
Where-on-Earth Bird Walk 

These walks will be held twice a month on Tuesdays. 

 

So what exactly is a Where-on-Earth Bird Walk?

 

It is a bird walk that we take individually on the same day (at any time of day), and make a list of birds that you each see. You send your list to Kim Gaffett at kim.gaffett@tnc.org along with information about the time of day and where you observed birds. Kim will compile everyone’s list into one “community” list, and share the sightings with all the participants – thus a bird walk that we do together, but at a social distance.

 

So, please join us – bird enthusiasts near & far and young & old – in taking your own Where-on-Earth Bird Walk  at any time that fits your schedule.  

 

Remember this is not limited to those who are presently on Block Island. Anyone, anywhere can join the fun. 

 

Any Questions, contact Kim at kim.gaffett@tnc.org

Programs are weather dependent

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2025 Storm Trysail Club Block Island Race Week
summary: The Storm Trysail Club (STC) is excited to announce the  31st Block Island Race Week (BIRW). Block Island Race Week 2025 will take place from June 22-27, in Rhode Island Sound at Champlain’s Marina and Resort, promising a thrilling mix of ORC and PHRF racing and a great post-racing atmosphere.
content:
The Storm Trysail Club (STC) is excited to announce the  31st Block Island Race Week (BIRW). Block Island Race Week 2025 will take place from June 22-27, in Rhode Island Sound at Champlain’s Marina and Resort, promising a thrilling mix of ORC and PHRF racing and a great post-racing atmosphere. 

 Known for its unique combination of distance and buoy racing, Block Island Race Week offers something for every sailor. Beyond the racing, the event is renowned for its outstanding hospitality, making it a must-attend on the sailing calendar. In 2023, the 30th edition of BIRW saw 174 boats compete, and the STC hopes to see even more participants in 2025.

title: 33rd Annual Holiday Shopping Stroll
summary: Do your holiday shopping right here on Block Island this year!
content:
Do your holiday shopping right here on Block Island this year!
Our downtown shops and outdoor holiday marketplace offer a wide array of unique gifts for everyone on your list. There will be food and drinks to fuel your Block Island shopping adventure.
Grab some friends and take a ferry ride to Block Island - or if you're already here come into town - for a festive, fun, and special holiday shopping experience. Don’t miss out on participating in the holiday stroll raffle!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Pride 2024
summary: Block Island is celebrating its Annual Block Island Pride! Starting on Thursday, September 12th through Saturday, September 14th. There will be many fun activities going on. Check out the event schedule below.
content:
Block Island is celebrating its Annual Block Island Pride! Starting on Thursday, September 12th through Saturday, September 14th. There will be many fun activities going on. Check out the event schedule below. 

 

Thursday, September 12 

Start your engines at the Rainbow Tie-Dye party at The Traveling Seamstress from 4:00-6:00 pm. Sling those colors like you mean it! Then, sashay your way to Drag Queen Bingo at The Island Free Library from 5:00-6:00 pm. Yasss, darling, come win some fabulous prizes! Cap off your night with a sultry Jazz Bar Speakeasy at Persephone's Kitchen from 6:00-8:30 pm. We’re talking smooth jazz and even smoother queens!

 

Friday, September 13

Get in touch with Mother Nature at Queerness in Nature with The Nature Conservancy from 12:00-2:00 pm. After that, unleash your inner artist at the Life Drawing Class from 4:00-5:15 pm (Location TBA, RSVP Required, 21+). When the sun sets, grab your popcorn and join us for Soundwaves' "To Wong Foo" at Town Beach at 6:30 pm. Then, get ready to party all night at Liquid Dancefloor Drag Party at Captain Nick's from 9:00 pm-1:00 am with beats by the one and only DJ Chardanaynay! (21+)

 

Saturday, September 14

Rise and shine for Drag Queen Story Hour at The Island Free Library from 10:00-11:00 am. Your favorite queens will be reading fierce stories and spreading glittery joy! At noon, strut your stuff in the Pride March starting at The Ocean View Pavilion. Bring your extravaganza and make some noise! After the march, get your brunch on at The Yellow Kittens from 1:30-4:00 pm with DJ Libre spinning the tunes. Family-friendly and absolutely fabulous! And for the grand finale, dance the night away at the Club Soda House & Techno Pride Rave from 10:00 pm-1:00 am with DJ Chardanaynay slaying the decks again! (21+)

And honey, all weekend long, you’ll be treated to spectacular performances from the iconic Ninny Nothin’ and her fabulous crew of drag queens. You won’t want to miss a single beat of this rainbow extravaganza!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Honoring the Block Island Manissean Indigenous Natives
summary: Honoring the Block Island Manissean Indigenous Natives:​​​​​​​​​On July 27 and 28 at the Block Island Historical Society, all are welcome to celebrate at our Intertribal Social to share the rich heritage of the original native Islanders through storytelling, cultural songs, drumming, and traditional dance exhibitions. Taste the flavors of traditional food and enjoy arts and crafts. Opening ceremony at 12 noon.
content:
Honoring the Block Island Manissean Indigenous Natives:​​​​​​​​​On July 27 and 28 at the Block Island Historical Society, all are welcome to celebrate at our Intertribal Social to share the rich heritage of the original native Islanders through storytelling, cultural songs, drumming, and traditional dance exhibitions. Taste the flavors of traditional food and enjoy arts and crafts. Opening ceremony at 12 noon.

Click the picture below for the full schedule of events.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tri-State Canyon Shootout - Fishing Tournament at Champlin's
summary: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. This year the tournament will be based out of Champlin's. So come down and check out the boats and the fish. The afternoon weigh-ins are not to be missed
content: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. author: Austin Crawford
title: First Annual Block Island Pirates Weekend!
summary: Ahoy, mateys! Prepare to set sail for the First Annual Block Island Pirates Weekend! Join us for a thrilling weekend brimming with adventure and excitement. Experience the majestic presence of the Oliver Hazard Perry ship, Pirate stories at the library for kids, embark on a daring Pirate Scavenger Hunt, and indulge in a savory Rum Dinner fit for buccaneers. Keep an eye out across the Island for roving pirates! Sign up for tickets to the BIMI Chowder Fest Sunday and take advantage of the great seafood specials our restaurants are preparing for the weekend. Don your finest pirate attire and join us for a weekend filled with unforgettable memories. Arrr you ready for the adventure of a lifetime? Don't miss out!
content:
Ahoy, mateys! Prepare to set sail for the First Annual Block Island Pirates Weekend! Join us for a thrilling weekend brimming with adventure and excitement. Experience the majestic presence of the Oliver Hazard Perry ship, Pirate stories at the library for kids, embark on a daring Pirate Scavenger Hunt, and indulge in a savory Rum Dinner fit for buccaneers. Keep an eye out across the Island for roving pirates! Sign up for tickets to the BIMI Chowder Fest Sunday and take advantage of the great seafood specials our restaurants are preparing for the weekend. Don your finest pirate attire and join us for a weekend filled with unforgettable memories. Arrr you ready for the adventure of a lifetime? Don't miss out!

 

Check calendar for the schedule of events.

title: Pirate Weekend  Kids Scavenger Hunt! All Day
